title: Today's AI Brief: Kiwibit’s AI-powered bird feeder is my new backyard buddy
summary: A look at Kiwibit's AI-powered bird feeder and OpenAI's guidance on third-party AI evaluations.

body.text: I'm excited to share some fresh AI news with you. Let's dive right in. I've been checking out Kiwibit's AI-powered bird feeder, and it's become my new favorite backyard accessory. The Kiwibit Bird Feeder Pro 4K AI Camera is pretty straightforward to set up, with multiple mounting options. What I find fascinating is how it's changing the way I interact with my backyard wildlife. I'm not just tossing seeds out there; I'm learning about the birds visiting my yard, and it's amazing.

The Kiwibit Bird Feeder Pro 4K AI Camera is making waves with its AI capabilities. It allows you to monitor and learn about the birds visiting your yard. I've been using it for a bit now, and the insights it's providing are quite impressive. I'm surprised by the variety of birds I have in my backyard. It's like having a personal bird guide. Plus, it's fun to watch the videos of the birds; my family and I have become bird enthusiasts overnight.

OpenAI recently shared guidance on third-party AI evaluations. This playbook covers how to assess model capabilities, safeguards, and validity for frontier systems. It's an interesting move, showing a commitment to transparency and safety in AI development. I'm curious to see how this will shape the industry. Ensuring AI systems are trustworthy and reliable is crucial as they become more integrated into our lives.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
